title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn
intro: Certain herbal teas can be beneficial for alleviating heartburn symptoms due to their natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Chamomile tea is a popular choice, as it can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, while also promoting relaxation and sleep. Ginger tea is another effective option, as it has been shown to aid in digestion, reduce stomach acid, and alleviate nausea. Slippery elm tea forms a protective layer on the inside of the esophagus, helping to prevent acid from backing up and causing discomfort. Lastly, licorice root tea can help increase mucus production in the stomach, which can protect the lining of the stomach and esophagus from acid. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before consuming large amounts of licorice root tea, as it can have side effects when taken in excess.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea provides relief from heartburn due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. This tea, popular for its soothing effects, contains antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, thus preventing acid from backing up. Furthermore, chamomile tea contains flavonoids like apigenin, which are known to aid in decreasing acid production in the stomach. Its calming effects also alleviate stress, which can often exacerbate heartburn symptoms. Lastly, the mildly bitter compound in chamomile, known as chamazulene, stimulates the digestive system, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced heartburn.
tea_parts: Flowers: Chamomile's daisy-like flowers contain azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by heartburn.
Leaves: The leaves of chamomile contain flavonoids that have been shown to help relax the smooth muscles of the esophagus, reducing the symptoms of heartburn.
Seeds: The seeds of chamomile are rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that can form a protective coating on the lining of the esophagus, helping to soothe and heal irritation from heartburn.

teas.tea_desc: Marshmallow tea provides relief from heartburn due to the demulcent properties of its main ingredient, the marshmallow root. This root contains mucilage, a substance that turns into a slippery gel when combined with water. This gel creates a protective barrier over the mucous membrane of the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux. As a result, marshmallow tea alleviates the discomfort of heartburn by coating the esophageal lining, neutralizing acid, and promoting healing. Furthermore, this tea's soothing effect on the gastrointestinal tract encourages proper digestion, helping to prevent future heartburn incidents.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, slippery substance that can coat and soothe inflamed mucous membranes, providing relief for heartburn.

Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing irritation in the esophagus and providing heartburn relief.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant are mucilaginous, providing a soothing and protective coating to the digestive tract, helping to prevent acid reflux and providing heartburn relief.

teas.tea_desc: Slippery elm tea provides relief for heartburn due to its demulcent properties, which involve the formation of a protective barrier in the digestive tract. This barrier prevents stomach acid from reaching and irritating the esophagus, thus alleviating the discomfort caused by heartburn. Moreover, slippery elm's soothing mucilage content increases the viscosity of digestive fluids, further minimizing acid exposure in the gastrointestinal system. Consequently, this herbal remedy attenuates inflammation and promotes faster healing of damaged tissues. Lastly, the anti-inflammatory and acid-buffering capabilities of slippery elm tea make it an effective natural solution for managing symptoms associated with heartburn.
tea_parts: Bark: The bark of the slippery elm plant contains high levels of mucilage, a gel-like substance that coats and soothes the digestive tract, making it an effective ingredient for medicinal tea to treat heartburn.
Leaves: Slippery elm leaves are a valuable addition to heartburn tea due to their demulcent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and mucilage, slippery elm seeds offer a potent soothing effect when brewed in a tea for heartburn, providing relief for stomach irritation.

Roots: Slippery elm roots have mucilaginous properties and have been traditionally used in teas to treat gastrointestinal issues, including heartburn, by forming a protective layer on the lining of the stomach and esophagus.

Inner Bark: The inner bark of the slippery elm tree contains high amounts of mucilage and tannins, which help alleviate heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation, coating the digestive tract, and promoting healing.

teas.tea_desc: Licorice tea brings immediate relief from heartburn due to its demulcent properties, which involve a softening or soothing effect on body tissues and membranes. This herbal tea contains glycyrrhizic acid, a compound that reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating the discomfort caused by acid reflux. Furthermore, licorice tea enhances the mucosal lining of the digestive tract, providing a protective barrier against harsh stomach acids that contribute to heartburn. Additionally, this tea stimulates the production of mucus, adding an extra layer of protection in the gut while promoting the healing of damaged tissues. Finally, licorice tea's antispasmodic properties relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing acid reflux and its associated heartburn sensation.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps balance stomach acid and soothes heartburn.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant have similar properties to the roots and are used to make tea that helps relieve heartburn symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant provide antioxidants and flavonoids that protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making it helpful for heartburn.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain useful compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, making it beneficial for heartburn relief.

teas.tea_desc: Cinnamon tea aids in heartburn relief due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. This spice's antioxidant qualities also help protect the gut lining from damage. Furthermore, cinnamon's natural ability to regulate stomach acid levels contributes to heartburn alleviation. Additionally, its calming effect on the digestive tract soothes muscle spasms and promotes healthy digestion. Lastly, cinnamon tea's warming sensation stimulates digestive enzymes, facilitating efficient breakdown of food and preventing acid build-up.
tea_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain compounds that are known to have antacid properties, providing relief from heartburn.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, they help balance stomach acidity, reducing the discomfort of heartburn.
Seeds: High in mucilage content, they form a protective layer in the stomach, preventing acid reflux and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Bark: Its astringent qualities help contract stomach tissues, reducing the incidence of acid reflux and providing heartburn relief.

teas.tea_desc: Ginger tea provides instant relief from heartburn by neutralizing stomach acids. Its anti-inflammatory properties address the root cause of heartburn, reducing irritation in the esophagus. Ginger tea's carminative effect relaxes the intestinal tract, preventing acid from backing up into the esophagus. Moreover, gingerols in ginger tea stimulate digestion, allowing food to move through the stomach more quickly, thereby reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Lastly, ginger tea's natural calming effect on the stomach lining alleviates heartburn symptoms, providing soothing comfort.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are a popular choice for medicinal tea to alleviate heartburn due to their natural anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the ginger plant are used in medicinal tea to soothe heartburn symptoms, as they contain compounds that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are known for their calming effects and are used in medicinal tea for heartburn relief, as they help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are rich in antioxidants and are often included in medicinal tea for heartburn, as they aid in digestion and reduce acid reflux.
Buds: The buds of the ginger plant have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a key ingredient in medicinal tea for heartburn relief.

teas.tea_desc: Cloves tea aids heartburn relief due to its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from eugenol, a potent compound in cloves. This compound helps by blocking stomach acid production, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, and providing pain relief for heartburn symptoms. Additionally, cloves tea promotes mucus production, forming a protective layer in the stomach lining, which further prevents acid reflux and resulting heartburn. Furthermore, cloves tea's antioxidant properties support the digestive system's overall health and functioning, alleviating heartburn prevalence. Consuming cloves tea after meals can help maintain a healthy digestive process, preventing heartburn incidents.
tea_parts: Buds: The clove buds contain eugenol, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing heartburn relief.
Flowers: Cloves flowers have flavonoids that aid in digestion and can help prevent the symptoms of heartburn.

Seeds: Cloves seeds contain compounds like acetyl eugenol and crategolic acid, which have gastroprotective and antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining, reducing the risk of heartburn.

teas.tea_desc: Turmeric tea relieves heartburn due to its active ingredient, curcumin, which boasts anti-inflammatory properties. This compound inhibits the production of stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Moreover, curcumin boosts the lining of the stomach, further preventing acid buildup. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant qualities repair cellular damage in the gastrointestinal tract. Finally, turmeric tea enhances digestion, allowing for smoother processing of food and beverages, minimizing heartburn symptoms.
tea_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of turmeric, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, helps soothe the esophagus and alleviate heartburn.
Roots: Rich in curcumin, turmeric roots aid in digestion and reduce inflammation, providing heartburn relief.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, turmeric leaves help repair damaged esophageal tissue and prevent heartburn.
Flowers: High in essential oils, turmeric flowers promote digestive health and provide instant heartburn relief.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds, with their natural antacid properties, neutralize stomach acid and prevent heartburn.

teas.tea_desc: Dandelion tea enjoys a long-standing reputation as a heartburn remedy due to its natural properties. This herbal tea contains substances that encourage production of digestive juices, facilitating breakdown of food and preventing acid buildup. Moreover, dandelion boasts anti-inflammatory qualities, which alleviate irritation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. Its bitterness stimulates the release of bile from the liver, aiding overall digestion and easing heartburn symptoms. Finally, dandelion tea's diuretic effect promotes the flushing of excess stomach acid, further contributing to heartburn relief.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions contain nutrients and compounds that support digestion and reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate heartburn.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in antioxidants and have natural diuretic properties, helping to cleanse the liver, reduce inflammation, and soothe heartburn.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers are a source of antioxidants and can stimulate digestion, reducing symptoms of heartburn and promoting overall gut health.

teas.tea_desc: Milk thistle tea brings relief to heartburn sufferers due to its natural ingredients, primarily silymarin. This flavonoid compound prevents the stomach acid from rising to irritate the esophagus, thus stopping heartburn. Moreover, milk thistle's antioxidant properties protect the tissues of the digestive tract, promoting their healing. Lastly, its anti-inflammatory effects further soothe the inflamed esophageal lining, ensuring long-term heartburn alleviation.
tea_parts: Seeds: The seeds of milk thistle contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex with antioxidant properties that protect the liver and support digestion, making it helpful for heartburn relief.
Leaves: The leaves of milk thistle are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, providing soothing relief for inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: Milk thistle flowers have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and minimize heartburn.
